thesis_anchors_verified: Per chapter-2 thesis: 'duty is not optional, even when it is hard' — 2.33 carries the if-then form of this directly: if you do not perform this duty, three losses follow.

Per chapter-2 thesis: 'do not flatten this into follow-your-passion' — 2.33's imam-scoped specificity makes it impossible to flatten the verse to passion-talk; the duty is in lane, in motion, un-pickup-able.
Per chapter-2 thesis: voice is 'didactic, corrective, slightly stern' — 2.33 voice is appropriately sharp, naming the failure-mode without softening.
Per chapter-2 thesis: 'the actor sits inside a larger order' — 2.33 honours this through the team / organisational-debt framing.
ordering_check: 2.33 correctly follows 2.32. 2.32 named the unsought-arrival as marker; 2.33 names the if-then conditional: if you do not act on the duty already arrived, here are the consequences. 2.34 then develops the kīrti-loss into avyayām akīrtim. The block has clear progressive logic.
verdict_reason: Verse supports thesis directly and is correctly positioned. PASS on both 10.3 and 10.4.
